source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/참조 문서/3. Usability (IEC 62366)/Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. UVR-01, Rev.0).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Validation Report according to IEC 62366 for a radiographic imaging device, documenting summative usability test methods, goals, results, and conclusion.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports usability engineering validation by documenting participant testing, acceptance criteria, task completion results, residual usability risk conclusions, and referenced standards.
keyPoints: The device is intended to obtain information necessary for patient diagnosis through anatomical structure images obtained using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR, or DR portable flat panel.
A total of 7 participants were involved in the usability test.
The usability goals were 100% completion rate and below 5 errors for each usability task.
ACT #1 through ACT #10 passed with 100% task completion.
The report concludes residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: A usability test is intended to determine the extent an interface facilitates a user’s ability to complete routine tasks.

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: The summative evaluation has been performed according to:

concepts.name: Task completion
concepts.evidence: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal

concepts.name: Use error
concepts.evidence: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

concepts.name: Residual usability risk
concepts.evidence: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Usability Validation Report.
The document is according to IEC 62366.
The document approval/preparation dates shown are 2018.02.10.
Revision number 0 was established by manufacturer’s Risk Management Policy on 2016.07.31.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Established by manufacturer’s Risk Management Policy 2016.07.31

evidence.claim: The device intended use is to obtain diagnostic information through anatomical structure images from radiation exposure using cassette IP, CR, or DR portable flat panel.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

evidence.claim: Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants was involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.

evidence.claim: Participants included persons educated about the medical device and licensed equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receive education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: The usability completion rate goal was 100%.
evidence.evidenceText: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal

evidence.claim: The usability error goal was below 5 errors for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: A total errors of below 5 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: ACT #1 through ACT #10 each had 100% test report results.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1) Acceptance criteria Method description Test report 100% ... ACT #10) Acceptance criteria Method description Test report 100%

evidence.claim: Task completion for ACT #1 through ACT #10 passed with 100%.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion ACT #1 ACT #2 ACT #3 ACT #4 ACT #5 ACT #6 ACT #7 ACT #8 ACT #9 ACT #10 Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass 100 % Pass

evidence.claim: The report states that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2007, EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015, and EN 62366:2008.

reviewFlags.reason: Product name and model name fields are blank or represented as placeholder text in the source text.
FDA appears in source metadata/path authority signal but not in the body of the source text.
Document date appears in preparation/review/approval date fields, while revision history also lists 2016.07.31 for Rev.0 establishment.
Some table structure appears flattened in preprocessing, reducing confidence in exact association of roles, dates, and result columns.
